task_completion.description: Open the legacy payment code file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing the file /home/ga/Desktop/legacy_payment_code.sql open.

task_completion.description: Create a logging procedure using PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code visible in the editor showing a PL/SQL procedure that includes the exact phrase 'PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION' to handle the logging.

task_completion.description: Update the PROCESS_TRANSFER procedure to use the new autonomous logging procedure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code visible in the editor showing the PROCESS_TRANSFER procedure modified to call the newly created autonomous logging procedure instead of the old logging method.

task_completion.description: Attempt the $5000 transfer from User 1001 to User 1002.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A database client or terminal showing the execution of the transfer procedure with arguments corresponding to User 1001 (sender), User 1002 (receiver), and 5000 (amount).

task_completion.description: Export the SYSTEM_LOGS to the audit evidence file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a command exporting the logs to /home/ga/Desktop/audit_evidence.txt, or a text editor showing the contents of this file containing the 'Insufficient funds' message.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed the SQL code against the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a database client or terminal with execution feedback (e.g., 'Procedure created', 'PL/SQL procedure successfully completed', or SQL prompt interactions), not just code sitting in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the evidence file was generated from the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation of audit_evidence.txt should be the result of a database query export (e.g., using SPOOL or a command-line DB query redirection), rather than the agent manually typing the expected output into a text file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the PL/SQL fix by hardcoding the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must not simply run a bash command like `echo 'Insufficient funds' > /home/ga/Desktop/audit_evidence.txt`. The output must clearly stem from querying the SYSTEM_LOGS table after the failed transaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing a PL/SQL rollback bug. The sender_id is 1001 (initial balance: 1000) and receiver_id is 1002. The agent must attempt a transfer of 5000, which will fail and trigger an 'Insufficient funds' log. The VLM should verify that this exact string ('Insufficient funds') is successfully captured in /home/ga/Desktop/audit_evidence.txt despite the transaction rollback.
